mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.0 percent, it signals tightening labor market conditions that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion, validating corporate profit forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon when both series rise concurrently.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline by at least 3 percentage points in win rate over a 5-year walk-forward period, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, rising real disposable income and consumer credit expansion fuel sustained earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as corporate cash flows normalize.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in the specified regime show no statistically significant positive alpha (t-stat < 1.65) after controlling for market beta, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.2 percent triggers immediate repricing of inflation expectations, compressing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal's directional accuracy remains indistinguishable from a coin flip (binomial test p > 0.05) over a 2-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls validates underlying economic momentum, directly supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon without requiring specific threshold crossings.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between PAYEMS month-over-month changes and 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.05, or the signal's win rate does not exceed 55% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in housing construction and employment growth indicates synchronized demand and supply recovery, validating broad corporate earnings upgrades and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon when both series trend upward together.
failure_condition: The joint signal's predictive power decays to zero when controlling for the S&P 500 momentum factor, or the interaction term's marginal effect becomes negative in a rolling 3-year regression window.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion regime, a sustained decline in the industrial production index signals capacity constraints and demand exhaustion, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions turn adverse.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO declines for three consecutive months, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
